behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at the boundary between git-history analysis, age-threshold logic, and PR-presence lookup; unit tests of any one piece can miss wiring mistakes.
behaviors.test_description: Create an integration test with a local fixture repo plus a PR-state provider fake. Build branches with commits ahead of main at controlled commit dates, set threshold, return no open PR, and assert detector returns stale-no-pr only when all three conditions hold.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Risk is orchestration: session bootstrap must call detector and formatter and surface output in startup reporting. Single-function tests would miss dropped wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start initialization in a test harness with detector inputs indicating stale current branch, and assert emitted warning includes branch name, age, and next action text.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: This is pure mapping logic with no I/O; failures are local branching/enum handling defects.
behaviors.test_description: Table-drive classifier inputs to expected labels, including edge combinations and unknown-state guard behavior, and assert deterministic outputs across repeated calls.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real repo state and live PR state; mocks can hide real failures in git/CLI/API interaction and rendering path.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` end-to-end against a real test repository and real PR backend state, with one branch clean and one branch ahead-without-PR, then assert rendered status lines show `clean` vs `STALE` correctly.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is state-join logic between branch analysis and PR lifecycle classification (open/merged/none), which is a module-boundary concern.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test detector with branch states plus PR metadata fixtures covering open, merged, closed-unmerged, and none; assert only no-PR eligible branches can be stale-no-pr.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is git-history mutation behavior (rebase/squash) that is hard to faithfully mock; commit ancestry and reachability should be validated with real git operations.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real temporary git repo: create stale candidate branch, then simulate rebase and squash-merge flows that rewrite hashes while integrating work into main; rerun detector and assert branch is no longer stale-no-pr.
